page: Microsoft Azure E16ds_v7 is an Edsv7-series virtual machine configured with 16 dedicated vCPUs and 128.0 GB of system memory, yielding an 8.0 GB memory-to-core ratio. The underlying hardware features an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor operating at 4.2 GHz, utilizing an x86_64 architecture with 8 physical cores and 2 threads per core. This instance includes a 480 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for tile and int8 operations. Local storage is bundled with a 945 GB temporary disk, and the hypervisor layer is managed by Microsoft Hyper-V.

Benchmark data reveals strong single-core CPU performance, with stress-ng single-core metrics placing in the top 25%. Single-threaded compression and decompression tasks show top-tier execution, with gzip compression reaching 36.52 GB/sec and bzip3 decompression reaching 15.37 GB/sec, both placing in the top 10%. For machine learning workloads, the processor's AMX instructions assist in text generation for small (135M) models, achieving a top-tier result of 343.48 tokens/sec. Multi-core performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations fall within the average range, with Redis SET operations registering at 32.24 million operations per second.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the E16ds_v7 offers a high memory-to-vCPU density combined with dedicated compute resources and bundled local storage, reducing the need for external storage attachments for temporary data. However, a tradeoff exists in multi-core scaling, where multi-threaded benchmarks and memory bandwidth metrics remain in the average tier despite the high single-core capabilities. This server configuration is suited for memory-intensive workloads, relational databases, caching layers, and single-threaded data processing tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure E16ds_v7 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 16 dedicated vCPUs and 128.0 GB of RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor running at 4.2 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it includes 8 physical cores, a 480 MB L3 cache, and a bundled 945 GB local storage disk. The processor supports advanced instruction sets, including AVX-512 and Intel AMX. In benchmark testing, the instance demonstrates strong single-core CPU performance and top-tier single-threaded compression and decompression speeds, alongside top-tier text generation speeds for small language models. Conversely, multi-core performance and memory bandwidth remain in the average tier. This resource profile is suited for memory-intensive applications, database hosting, and caching workloads.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E16ds_v7 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 16 dedicated vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and a 945 GB local disk, delivering strong single-core performance and top-tier single-threaded compression.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E16ds_v7 is a memory-optimized VM with 16 vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and a 945 GB local disk, powered by a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon processor.
tagline: An x86_64 memory-optimized virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel Xeon compute, bundled local storage, and strong single-core processing capabilities.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated vCPUs and 128.0 GB RAM providing an 8:1 memory-to-core ratio.
Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor with 4.2 GHz clock speed and 480 MB L3 cache.
Bundled 945 GB local storage disk for temporary data requirements.
Top-tier single-threaded compression and small-model text generation benchmarks.
Suited for memory-intensive databases, caching layers, and data processing.
